framework.name: RFC 8693 token exchange fundamentally broken for multi-hop agents
framework.description: The draft explicitly states that OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange requires a synchronous round-trip to the AS at EACH delegation hop, making it architecturally unsuitable for agent chains. The act claim nesting in RFC 8693 is 'informational for access control decisions rather than a cryptographically self-verifiable attenuation chain.' This directly answers gap_2 — recursive delegation via token exchange doesn't work because the AS mediates each grant independently with no mechanism to ensure downstream consistency.
framework.raw_quote: RFC8693 supports representing prior delegation actors via nested act claims, but those claims are informational for access control decisions rather than a cryptographically self-verifiable attenuation chain. The AS mediates each grant independently, and no mechanism ensures that downstream delegation intent remains consistent with the original authorization scope.

source.title: Attenuating Authorization Tokens for Agentic Delegation Chains
